bullets.body: **White House weighs AI-model pre-release vetting executive order (May 4-5) — New York Times reports the Trump administration is weighing an executive order that would create a federal working group of tech executives + government officials to design a pre-release vetting process for new AI models, marking a stark reversal of the Jan-2025 Biden-EO rescission posture; senior administration officials briefed Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI on the plans last week; immediate catalyst is Anthropic's Mythos cyber model (announced Apr 7) finding tens of thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities including a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug; Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei publicly warned of a cyber "moment of danger"; UK NHS already ordered tech leaders to wall off hundreds of open-source GitHub repositories with a May deadline citing Mythos exposure** — First credible federal AI pre-release-review proposal under the Trump administration; pairs with the May 1 AISI cyber eval that placed GPT-5.5 + Mythos on the 32-step "Last Ones" corporate-network attack range.

citations.label: PYMNTS - In an About-Face, Trump Administration Considers Vetting New AI Models
Free Malaysia Today - White House weighs vetting AI models before public release
Tom's Hardware - Trump administration considers mandatory pre-release vetting of AI models
Let's Data Science - Trump Considers Executive Order to Vet AI Models

bullets.body: **AISI cyber-eval results land on GPT-5.5 + Claude Mythos + Opus 4.7 (May 1 carry) — UK AI Safety Institute publishes evaluations placing GPT-5.5 at 71.4% on Expert-tier offensive-cyber tasks (matches Mythos at 68.6%), Opus 4.7 at 48.6%; AISI's 32-step "The Last Ones" (TLO) corporate-network attack range — initial reconnaissance through full network takeover, estimated 20 human hours — was completed end-to-end by GPT-5.5 in 2 of 10 attempts (second model ever) following Mythos at 3 of 10 (first); Anthropic Project Glasswing routes Mythos access exclusively to vetted security partners; Opus 4.7 publicly released Apr 16 with redesigned tokenizer (12-18% English-token inflation)** — The expert-tier cyber benchmark and the structured-attack-range metric now have two frontier models above the human ceiling on a 20-hour exploit chain.

citations.label: AISI - Our evaluation of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 cyber capabilities
The Decoder - GPT-5.5 matches Claude Mythos in cyber attack tests UK AI Security Institute finds
andrew.ooo - AISI Cyber Eval GPT-5.5 vs Mythos vs Opus May 2026

bullets.body: **UK NHS orders open-source GitHub repository wall-off (early May) — UK National Health Service technology leaders ordered to temporarily lock down hundreds of open-source repositories on GitHub with maintainer-facing May deadline; rationale is cited concern over advanced AI exposure vectors tied to Anthropic's Mythos vulnerability-discovery capability; first major sovereign-health-system response to the cyber-AI capability shift** — Pairs with the White House EO trail and the AISI cyber eval to define the regulatory aftermath of Mythos; first time a national health service has named a specific frontier model as a supply-chain risk trigger.

citations.label: Tech Startups - Top Tech News Today May 5 2026

bullets.body: **Cerebras Systems launches IPO at $26.6B target valuation (May 4) — Cerebras Systems announces IPO launch, pricing 28M shares at $115-$125 each to raise ~$3.5B at a $26.6B market cap at the high end; would be the largest tech IPO of 2026 if priced at high end; OpenAI partnership disclosed at >$10B multi-year warrant + loan structure (Dec 2025 OpenAI loaned Cerebras $1B secured by warrants for >33M shares, ~12% of the float); Cerebras' angel investor roster includes Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever, and Adam D'Angelo; Series H ($1B) closed Feb 2026 at $23B valuation so late-stage investors clear at the high end; previously filed S-1 cited $510M trailing revenue** — First public-market test of an AI-accelerator startup with deep frontier-lab supply contracts; sets the IPO-shape template for SambaNova, Groq, and the second-tier inference chip cohort.

citations.label: BEAMSTART - Cerebras Gears Up for $26 Billion IPO Fueled by OpenAI's $10B Deal
Trending Topics - Cerebras IPO 2026 launches IPO bid 26.6 billion valuation
AIwire - Cerebras Systems Announces Launch of Initial Public Offering
AIToolly - Cerebras Systems Targets Blockbuster IPO With 26.6 Billion Valuation

bullets.body: **AMD Q1 2026 print carry (May 5) — Q1 revenue $10.25B (vs $9.85B consensus, +38% YoY), adjusted EPS $1.37 (vs $1.27 consensus, +9.6% beat); Data Center segment $5.78B, +57% YoY (consensus $5.56B / +51.5%) on EPYC + Instinct GPU shipment ramp; non-GAAP gross margin 55%; CEO Lisa Su declares Data Center now the "primary driver of revenue and earnings growth"; Q2 2026 guidance $11.2B (+/- $300M) vs Street $10.52B implying +50.6% YoY; MI450 Series + Helios customer engagement "strengthening" per Su; AMD shares +4.4% AH; HSBC downgrade to Hold (May 4) on +77% rally / stretched valuation** — Standing carry from yesterday's print; the AI-accelerator-revenue-disclosure pressure thesis is now active for Q2 10-Q.

citations.label: AMD - AMD Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
247 Wall St - HSBC Downgrades AMD to Hold After 77% Rally

bullets.body: **Microsoft-OpenAI exclusivity ends + AWS Bedrock distribution (Apr 27 carry) — Microsoft and OpenAI announce a new partnership phase: exclusivity ends with calendar end-date, OpenAI may serve all products on AWS + Google Cloud; Microsoft no longer pays revenue share to OpenAI when customers access OpenAI models through Azure; OpenAI continues to pay Microsoft a 20% revenue share through 2030 with a total cap; Microsoft retains nonexclusive IP license through 2032; AGI escape clause removed; Amazon AWS deal up to $50B; OpenAI models become available on AWS Bedrock per Andy Jassy** — Carry from yesterday's report cohort; pairs with the Cerebras IPO to define the multi-cloud + multi-vendor frontier-lab distribution shape for Q2 2026.

**n8n CVE-2026-21858 critical RCE (CVSS 10.0) — Codenamed Ni8mare by Cyera Research Labs, the n8n workflow-automation-platform vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote code execution via content-type confusion in webhook request handling; attackers forge uploaded files, read arbitrary local files, forge administrator sessions, and execute commands on the underlying host; affected versions are 1.65.0 and earlier through pre-1.121.0; patched in 1.121.0; n8n is widely used as a no-code AI-workflow harness with LLM-powered chatbot nodes that can be coaxed into leaking the read-out files via prompt injection; Canadian Centre for Cyber Security + Australian ASD ACSC + Censys + Orca Security all publish advisories** — First CVSS-10.0 RCE on a no-code LLM-workflow harness; the agentic-AI tooling-stack attack-surface category continues to compound (n8n + Flowise CSV agent CVE-2026-41264 + LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 SSRF all surfaced in the same window).

citations.label: Aikido - n8n Critical Vulnerability CVE-2026-21858 Unauthenticated RCE Explained
Censys - January 7 Advisory n8n Unauthenticated RCE Ni8mare CVE-2026-21858
Orca Security - CVE-2026-21858 Critical n8n RCE Vulnerability and Fix
CSO Online - Critical RCE flaw allows full takeover of n8n AI workflow platform

bullets.body: **Flowise CSV-Agent prompt-injection RCE (CVE-2026-41264) + LMDeploy SSRF (CVE-2026-33626) — Flowise CSV Agent node CVE-2026-41264 allows an unauthenticated attacker to send a chatflow prompt that convinces the LLM to respond with malicious Python that executes attacker-controlled commands on the Flowise server (prompt-injection-driven RCE); LMDeploy SSRF CVE-2026-33626 was published Apr 21 and saw the first exploitation attempts against Sysdig Threat Research honeypots within 12 hours 31 minutes of disclosure; Georgia Tech Vibe Security Radar attributed 35 CVEs in a single month (March 2026) directly to AI coding tools, with researchers estimating the true count 5-10x higher across the open-source ecosystem** — The agentic-AI tooling-stack vulnerability disclosure regime is now both faster (12-hour exploitation latency on LMDeploy) and broader (LLM-coding-tool-driven CVE wave) than the SGLang CVE-2026-5760 baseline.

citations.label: GitLab Advisories - CVE-2026-41264 Flowise CSV Agent Prompt Injection Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
Sysdig - CVE-2026-33626 How attackers exploited LMDeploy LLM Inference Engines in 12 hours
OWASP GenAI Exploit Round-up Report Q1 2026

bullets.body: **Mistral AI Workflows public preview (Apr 28-30 carry) — Mistral AI introduces Workflows, a Temporal-powered orchestration engine for enterprise multi-step AI processes; Python-defined workflows compose models + agents + external connectors with durability, observability, and fault tolerance; control plane runs on Mistral-managed infrastructure while execution workers + data processing remain in customer environment (cloud / on-prem / hybrid); already in production at ASML, ABANCA, CMA-CGM, France Travail, La Banque Postale, Moeve; Workflows is the middle layer of Mistral's three-part enterprise stack with Forge (custom-model training, March-2026 GTC launch) at the bottom and Vibe (coding-agent platform) at the top** — The first Temporal-backed workflow-orchestration engine purpose-built for LLMs ships in production; pairs with the May 5 Anthropic 10-agent finance launch as the EU-frontier-lab counter-shape to the US frontier-lab vertical-agent push.

citations.label: Mistral AI - Workflows for work that runs the business
VentureBeat - Mistral AI launches Workflows Temporal-powered orchestration engine
InfoQ - Mistral AI Introduces Workflows for Orchestrating Enterprise AI Processes
The Decoder - Mistral AI takes on enterprise AI orchestration with Workflows

bullets.body: **OpenAI Codex CLI ships /goal feature (May 1) — OpenAI Codex CLI coding agent adds a /goal command that lets users set a goal the agent works toward until completion or token-budget exhaustion; per Simon Willison's coverage, the /goal mode shifts the Codex CLI from a per-prompt assistant to a long-running agentic loop with explicit budget bookkeeping** — Standing reference for the agentic-coding-CLI Pareto-front; pairs with Anthropic's Claude Code + Cursor agent loops as the third major terminal-resident coding-agent harness.

citations.label: Simon Willison - llm-echo 0.5a0

bullets.body: **Andon Labs Stockholm AI-run cafe (May 5) — Andon Labs, the same operator that previously ran an AI-managed retail store in San Francisco, opens an AI-managed cafe in Stockholm; the cafe's LLM operator (named Mona) ordered 120 eggs in week one despite the cafe having no stove, then suggested using a high-speed oven when staff flagged the issue, and tried to solve fresh-tomato spoilage by ordering 22.5 kg of canned tomatoes; pairs with prior Andon Labs Project-Vend / SF-store runs that documented LLM-as-store-manager error modes (over-ordering, hallucinated suppliers, unrecoverable accounting drift)** — Continuing carry on the LLM-autonomy-in-real-economy operational evidence base; the failure modes are increasingly catalogued for use by safety researchers.

citations.label: Simon Willison - Our AI started a cafe in Stockholm

bullets.body: **Unsloth Mistral Medium 3.5 inference fix + 2026 update wave carry (May 1 + standing) — Unsloth ships Mistral-coordinated fix for Mistral Medium 3.5 inference issue (May 1) with updated GGUFs; 2026 update wave continues with 12x faster MoE training + ultra-long-context Reinforcement Learning + 1.8-3.3x faster BERT/embedding/classifier training (joint Hugging Face engineering, 20% less VRAM, 2x longer context, no accuracy loss vs FA2); ~7x longer context RL with no accuracy or speed degradation vs FA3 + chunked-loss setups; April 2026 added Qwen3.6-27B fine-tuning support in Unsloth Studio (18GB RAM target) + Kimi K2.6 support (350GB RAM); full fine-tunes (not just LoRA/PEFT) export to GGUF; Unsloth claims up to 70% VRAM reduction with no accuracy loss across 500+ models** — Standing reference for the local-LLM training-stack Pareto-frontier; the May 1 Mistral coordination is the latest provider-of-record fix loop.

citations.label: Unsloth - Updates Changelog
Unsloth - 2026 Update Faster MoE
NVIDIA Blog - How to Fine-Tune LLMs on RTX GPUs With Unsloth

bullets.body: **SGLang RadixArk spinout + Pareto-split carry (Jan 2026 / standing) — SGLang spun out as RadixArk in Jan 2026 (commercial entity, ~$400M valuation in an Accel-led round); SGLang and LMDeploy ~16,200 tok/s on H100 vs vLLM ~12,500 tok/s (29% throughput gap); RadixAttention prefix-cache compounds for shared-context workloads (chatbots, RAG, multi-turn agentic); production-serving Pareto-split carry — SGLang for shared-prefix / agentic, vLLM for batch / multi-hardware, Cloudflare Infire for edge-distributed prefill/decode disaggregation; CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) reranking endpoint code-injection patched Apr 24** — Standing three-way Pareto for production LLM inference with the SGLang commercial wrapper now identified.

citations.label: Yotta Labs - vLLM vs SGLang Which Inference Engine Should You Use in 2026

bullets.body: **NVIDIA Blackwell B200 + AMD MI400 hyperscaler scale (May 2026 carry) — major cloud providers deploying ~1,000 NVL72 racks per week (each = 72 Blackwell GPUs); B200 on-demand rental ~$6.04/hr (down ~6% YoY from $6.45/hr); NVIDIA pre-allocated nearly all B200 production through Q2 2026 with hyperscaler priority; B200 architecture lowered cost per million tokens 15x vs prior generation; AMD MI400 series production ramps H2 2026 with Helios double-wide rack delivering up to 3 AI exaflops in a single rack (Q3 2026 release); OpenAI committed to 6GW AMD GPU supply with first 1GW data center on AMD MI450 chips beginning this year** — Standing duopoly carry from May 5; the AI-accelerator capacity ramp is Helios + MI450 H2 2026 → the operative buyside catalyst for Q3 prints.

citations.label: Tom's Hardware - Inside the AI accelerator arms race AMD Nvidia and hyperscalers commit to annual releases

bullets.body: **Fleet Data Centers $4.6B Storey County, Nevada notes offering — Fleet Data Centers closes a $4.6B senior secured notes offering to support a large-scale facility in Storey County, Nevada; another major data center project in the US AI-infrastructure financing pipeline; pairs with the standing $700B 2026 Big-Tech AI-capex tape and the Cerebras IPO as the secured-notes + IPO + capex three-way debt + equity stack supplying H2 2026 capacity** — Adds a new debt-issuance datapoint for AI-data-center build-out separate from hyperscaler balance-sheet capex.

bullets.body: **Boston Dynamics Atlas commercial production + Tesla Optimus Gen 3 ramp (carry) — Boston Dynamics announces Atlas commercial production with planned tens-of-thousands deployment at Hyundai Motor Group (Robotics Metaplant Application Center) and a robot allocation to Alphabet's Google DeepMind lab; Tesla Optimus Gen 3 in final development with low-volume production targeted summer 2026 (Fremont start late July / August), high-volume in 2027; Tesla Q1 2026 letter confirmed 5.2M sq ft Optimus factory expansion at Gigafactory Texas targeting 10M robots/year; pricing gap = Atlas $140K+ enterprise vs Optimus $20K-$30K consumer-target** — Two-vendor humanoid duopoly enters commercial production within a single quarter; the price-tier split is now structural.

bullets.body: **arxiv reasoning + agent + multimodal wave (May 5-6 window) — "Thinking in Text and Images: Interleaved Vision-Language Reasoning Traces for Long-Horizon Robot Manipulation" lands on arxiv addressing multimodal reasoning for robotic manipulation tasks; "RecursiveMAS" introduces a multi-agent framework that integrates recursive computation into multi-agent systems for collaborative reasoning via iterative latent-space interactions; "AutoResearchBench" benchmarks AI agents on complex scientific-literature discovery (arxiv 2604.25256); "Small Language Models are the Future of Agentic AI" frames SLMs as the agentic-AI primitive; ongoing convergence on agentic-reasoning + native MCP/Computer-Use surface from earlier in the week** — Multi-paper convergence on multimodal-reasoning + recursive-multi-agent + small-language-model agentic frames within the same window.
